name: Home Alone
description: The 1990 Christmas comedy that made Macaulay Culkin a star and booby-traps a family tradition.

questions.q: Who plays 8-year-old Kevin McCallister in Home Alone?

source.notes: Culkin was 10 at release; film made him the highest-paid child star of the era.

questions.q: Who plays Kevin's mother Kate, delivering the iconic 'KEVIN!' scream?

source.notes: O'Hara had a long career with Christopher Guest and Schitt's Creek.

questions.q: The McCallister family is flying to which European capital for Christmas when they leave Kevin behind?

source.notes: Paris scenes were actually shot in Paris.

questions.q: Which writer/producer wrote and produced Home Alone — the same man behind many 1980s teen comedies?

source.notes: Hughes later did Home Alone 2: Lost in New York.

questions.q: Which two actors play the 'Wet Bandits,' the bumbling burglars targeting the McCallister house?

source.notes: Harry Lime (Pesci) and Marv Merchants (Stern).

questions.q: What's the nickname of the scruffy old neighbor Kevin initially fears?

source.notes: Played by Roberts Blossom.

questions.q: What classic black-and-white movie does Kevin use to trick a pizza delivery guy into thinking there's a gangster in the house?

source.notes: 'Angels with Filthy Souls' — a fictional pastiche created for the film.

questions.q: Who directed Home Alone, before going on to launch the Harry Potter film franchise?

source.notes: Columbus directed the first two Harry Potter films.

questions.q: In what year was Home Alone released in theaters?

source.notes: Released November 16, 1990.

questions.q: Which iconic composer scored Home Alone, writing the now-famous 'Somewhere in My Memory'?

source.notes: The score was Oscar-nominated.

questions.q: Home Alone set a box-office record for which specific achievement?
choices: Highest-grossing live-action comedy ever (at the time)
Longest #1 box-office run by a holiday film

source.notes: Record held until 2011.

questions.q: Which comedian plays polka-band leader Gus Polinski, who gives Kate McCallister a ride home?

source.notes: Candy filmed the entire part in about 23 hours as a favor to Hughes.

questions.q: Who plays Kevin's bullying older brother Buzz?

source.notes: Devin Ratray reprised the role in sequels.

questions.q: What's the name of the scene-stealing younger cousin who enjoys Pepsi?

source.notes: Played by Macaulay's real-life younger brother Kieran Culkin.

questions.q: What Christmas decoration does Kevin decorate with life-sized mannequins, baby dolls, and a toy train to fake a party?
choices: A moving display of dancing figures

source.notes: Kevin pulls strings on a model train rig with cardboard cutouts.

questions.q: Which aggressive lawn-based weapon does Kevin set up on the front steps to slip Harry?

source.notes: The icy porch steps are one of the first booby traps.

questions.q: What name does the cartoonish burglar duo tag the homes they rob, giving themselves their nickname?

source.notes: They leave sinks running to flood the house.

questions.q: Which 1992 sequel reunites the same main cast, sending Kevin to New York City?
choices: Home Alone 2: Lost in New York
Home Alone 2: Christmas in the City

source.notes: Also directed by Chris Columbus.

questions.q: Which future U.S. president had a brief cameo at the Plaza Hotel in Home Alone 2?

source.notes: Trump appears directing Kevin to the lobby.

questions.q: Which actor was John Hughes's first choice for Harry before Joe Pesci was cast?

source.notes: De Niro also turned down later Ghostbusters roles.

questions.q: Home Alone's concept is said to have grown out of a scene in which earlier John Hughes/John Candy film?

source.notes: Uncle Buck's door-peephole interrogation scene.

questions.q: Which country still airs Home Alone every Christmas Eve as a national broadcast tradition?

source.notes: Polish public TV's annual Christmas Eve airing is now an institution.
